info.title: Sim Tables API v2
info.description: Version 2 of the Sim Tables API for managing tables, their column schemas, and rows of structured data. v2 standardizes every endpoint on a single response family: a single resource is returned as `{ data }`, lists are returned as `{ data, nextCursor }` with opaque cursor pagination, and errors are returned as `{ error: { code, message, details? } }`. Rate-limit state is carried in `X-RateLimit-*` response headers. Authenticate every request with the `X-API-Key` header. Row `data` is always keyed by column name.

tags.name: Tables
tags.description: Manage tables, columns, and rows for structured data storage (v2 API).

get.description: List all tables in a workspace. Returns the full bounded set of tables for the workspace as a single page, so `nextCursor` is always null.

post.description: Create a new table with a typed column schema. The schema must contain between 1 and 50 columns.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

requestBody.description: The table name, optional description, column schema, and target workspace.

get.description: Get a single table's metadata and column schema.

delete.description: Archive a table. Returns the id of the archived table.

post.description: Add a column to the table schema. Returns the table's full column list after the change.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL

requestBody.description: The workspace and the column definition to add.

patch.description: Update a column by name — rename it, change its type, or toggle its required/unique constraints. Provide at least one field in `updates`. Returns the table's full column list after the change.

requestBody.description: The workspace, the current column name, and the fields to change.

delete.description: Delete a column from the table schema by name. A table must always keep at least one column. Returns the table's full column list after the change.

requestBody.description: The workspace and the name of the column to delete.

get.description: Query rows from a table with optional filtering, sorting, and cursor pagination. `filter` and `sort` are passed as JSON-encoded query parameters and key on column names. Pagination uses an opaque cursor: pass the `nextCursor` from a previous response to fetch the next page; `nextCursor` is null on the final page. Total row count is available as `rowCount` on the table resource.

post.description: Insert one or many rows. Send a single-row body (`{ data }`) to insert one row, or a batch body (`{ rows }`) to insert up to 1000 rows in one request. The response shape mirrors the request: a single insert returns `{ data: { row } }`, a batch insert returns `{ data: { rows, insertedCount } }`. Row `data` is keyed by column name.

requestBody.description: Either a single-row payload or a batch payload.

put.description: Bulk-update every row matching a filter, applying the same partial `data` patch to each. The filter must contain at least one condition. `updatedRowIds` is always returned (empty when nothing matched).

requestBody.description: The workspace, a non-empty filter, the patch data, and an optional row cap.

delete.description: Delete rows in bulk, either by a non-empty filter or by an explicit list of row ids. Provide exactly one of `filter` or `rowIds`. For id-based deletes the response also reports `requestedCount` and any `missingRowIds`; these fields are omitted for filter-based deletes.

requestBody.description: The workspace and either a non-empty filter or an explicit list of row ids.

get.description: Get a single row by id.

patch.description: Partially update a single row by id. The `data` patch is keyed by column name and merges into the existing row.

requestBody.description: The workspace and the partial row data to apply.

delete.description: Delete a single row by id. Returns `deletedCount` and `deletedRowIds`, mirroring the bulk delete shape.

post.description: Insert a row, or update the existing row that conflicts on a unique column. When `conflictTarget` is omitted the server resolves the conflict against the table's single unique column. The response reports whether the row was inserted or updated.

requestBody.description: The workspace, the row data, and an optional unique column to resolve the conflict against.

200.description: The row was inserted or updated.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: Your Sim API key (personal or workspace). Generate one from the Sim dashboard under Settings > API Keys.

TableId.name: tableId
TableId.description: The unique identifier of the table.

RowId.name: rowId
RowId.description: The unique identifier of the row.

WorkspaceIdQuery.name: workspaceId
WorkspaceIdQuery.description: The unique identifier of the workspace that owns the table.

FilterQuery.name: filter
FilterQuery.description: JSON-encoded filter object keyed by column name. Supports equality ({"status": "active"}), comparison operators ({"age": {"$gt": 18}}), and $and/$or composition.

SortQuery.name: sort
SortQuery.description: JSON-encoded sort object mapping column name to direction. Example: {"created_at": "desc"}.

CursorQuery.name: cursor
CursorQuery.description: Opaque pagination cursor. Pass the `nextCursor` from a previous response to fetch the next page. Omit for the first page.

RateLimitLimit.description: Maximum number of requests permitted in the current rate-limit window.

RateLimitRemaining.description: Number of requests remaining in the current rate-limit window.

RateLimitReset.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the current rate-limit window resets.

RetryAfter.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

details.description: Optional structured error details, such as per-field validation issues.

Column.description: A column definition in a table schema.

id.description: Stable server-assigned column id. May be absent on legacy columns created before id backfill.

name.description: Column name. Starts with a letter or underscore; contains only alphanumerics and underscores.

required.description: Whether the column requires a value on insert.

unique.description: Whether values in this column must be unique across all rows.

workflowGroupId.description: Set when the column is the output of a workflow group.

ColumnInput.description: Column definition supplied when creating a table or adding a column.

name.description: Column name. Starts with a letter or underscore; contains only alphanumerics and underscores.

required.description: Whether the column requires a value on insert.

unique.description: Whether values in this column must be unique across all rows.

Table.description: A user-defined table with a typed column schema.

description.description: Optional description of the table. Null when not set.

columns.description: Array of column definitions for the table.

rowCount.description: Current number of rows in the table.

maxRows.description: Maximum rows allowed by the current billing plan.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the table was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the table was last modified.

RowData.description: Row cells keyed by column name. Each value is typed per its column definition.

Row.description: A single row in a table.

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the row was created.

updatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the row was last modified.

Filter.description: Filter object keyed by column name. Supports equality ({"status": "active"}), comparison operators ({"age": {"$gt": 18}}), and $and/$or composition. Must contain at least one condition.

CreateTableBody.description: Payload to create a new table.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that will own the table.

name.description: Table name. Starts with a letter or underscore; contains only alphanumerics and underscores.

columns.description: Column definitions. A table must have between 1 and 50 columns.

AddColumnBody.description: Payload to add a column to a table.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

name.description: Column name. Starts with a letter or underscore; contains only alphanumerics and underscores.

required.description: Whether the column requires a value on insert.

unique.description: Whether values in this column must be unique across all rows.

position.description: Zero-based insert position in the column order. Appended at the end when omitted.

UpdateColumnBody.description: Payload to update an existing column by name.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

columnName.description: The current name of the column to update.

updates.description: Fields to change. Provide at least one.

type.description: New data type for the column.

required.description: Whether the column requires a value on insert.

unique.description: Whether values in this column must be unique across all rows.

DeleteColumnBody.description: Payload to delete a column by name.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

columnName.description: The name of the column to delete.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

afterRowId.description: Insert directly after this row id. Mutually exclusive with beforeRowId.

beforeRowId.description: Insert directly before this row id. Mutually exclusive with afterRowId.

CreateRowBatchBody.description: Insert multiple rows in one request.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

rows.description: Rows to insert. Each entry is keyed by column name. Up to 1000 rows per request.

CreateRowsBody.description: Either a single-row payload or a batch payload.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching rows to update.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching rows to delete.

DeleteRowsByIdsBody.description: Delete an explicit list of rows by id.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

rowIds.description: Row ids to delete. Up to 1000 ids per request.

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to delete.

UpdateRowBody.description: Partial update for a single row.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

UpsertRowBody.description: Insert-or-update a row keyed by a unique column.

workspaceId.description: The workspace that owns the table.

conflictTarget.description: Name of the unique column to resolve the conflict against. When omitted, the server uses the table's single unique column.

TableEnvelope.description: A single table wrapped in the v2 data envelope.

TableListEnvelope.description: A page of tables. `nextCursor` is always null because the full bounded workspace set is returned in one page.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null when there are no more pages.

DeleteTableEnvelope.description: Confirmation that a table was archived.

id.description: The id of the archived table.

ColumnsEnvelope.description: The table's full column list after a column mutation.

RowEnvelope.description: A single row wrapped in the v2 data envelope.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null on the final page.

BatchInsertRowsEnvelope.description: Result of a batch row insert.

CreateRowsResponse.description: A single-row insert returns `{ data: { row } }`; a batch insert returns `{ data: { rows, insertedCount } }`.

updatedRowIds.description: Ids of the updated rows. Empty when nothing matched.

DeleteRowsEnvelope.description: Result of a bulk delete. `requestedCount` and `missingRowIds` are present only for id-based deletes.

requestedCount.description: Number of row ids requested. Present only for id-based deletes.

missingRowIds.description: Requested ids that did not exist. Present only for id-based deletes.

deletedCount.description: Always 1 when a row was deleted.

deletedRowIds.description: The id of the deleted row.

UpsertRowEnvelope.description: Result of an upsert, including whether the row was inserted or updated.

operation.description: Whether the row was inserted or updated.

BadRequest.description: Invalid request. The request body, query parameters, or a JSON-encoded filter/sort failed validation. Inspect `error.details` for field-level issues.

details.message: Table must have at least one column

Unauthorized.description: Invalid or missing API key. Ensure the X-API-Key header is set with a valid key.

Forbidden.description: Access denied. The API key cannot access the target workspace, or a plan limit (such as the maximum number of tables) has been reached.

NotFound.description: The requested table or row was not found. Verify the id is correct and belongs to the specified workspace.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded. Wait for the duration in the Retry-After header before retrying.
